nodes.name: e2e-runner.md
nodes.summary: Defines the E2E Test Runner agent responsible for planning, creating, and executing end-to-end tests using Agent Browser (primary) or Playwright (fallback), with structured workflow steps and flaky test handling strategies.

nodes.name: fastapi-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Defines the FastAPI code reviewer agent covering security, dependency injection, async patterns, and Pydantic validation with prioritized findings (critical/high/medium) and structured output format.

nodes.name: flutter-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive Flutter/Dart code review agent with an extensive checklist covering architecture, state management, widget composition, performance, accessibility, security, and internationalization across 25 structured sections.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers Flutter-specific patterns including BLoC/Provider/Riverpod state management, Dart null-safety idioms, and platform-channel integration.

nodes.name: fsharp-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: F# code review agent focusing on functional programming idioms, type safety, discriminated unions, computation expressions, and .NET interoperability with tiered priority findings and diagnostic commands.

nodes.name: gan-evaluator.md
nodes.summary: GAN (Generate-and-Evaluate) evaluator agent that systematically tests and scores generated web apps using browser automation, providing ruthlessly strict design and interaction quality feedback with structured scoring rubric.
nodes.languageNotes: Part of a three-agent GAN loop (planner/generator/evaluator) for iterative web app generation with multi-mode evaluation (playwright, screenshot, code-only).

nodes.name: gan-generator.md
nodes.summary: GAN generator agent that creates and iterates on web app implementations based on product specs and evaluator feedback, maintaining a generator state file and emphasizing creative quality to avoid AI-generated slop.
nodes.languageNotes: Pairs with gan-evaluator.md and gan-planner.md to form the three-part GAN agentic loop.

nodes.name: gan-planner.md
nodes.summary: GAN planner agent that translates user intent into a structured product specification, driving the downstream generator and evaluator agents in the iterative web app creation loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Entry point of the GAN (Generate-And-Evaluate) agentic trio alongside gan-generator.md and gan-evaluator.md.

nodes.name: go-build-resolver.md
nodes.summary: Go build error resolver agent providing diagnostic commands, module troubleshooting steps, and common fix patterns for compilation failures, with explicit stop conditions and structured output format.

nodes.name: go-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Go code review agent covering security, error handling, concurrency patterns, idiomatic Go style, and performance with tiered priority findings and diagnostic commands including go vet and staticcheck.

nodes.name: harmonyos-app-resolver.md
nodes.summary: HarmonyOS/ArkTS application development expert agent enforcing strict V2 state management and Navigation-only routing, with detailed ArkTS compilation constraint checks and API usage guidelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Targets HarmonyOS Next with ArkTS and ArkUI, enforcing ArkUI State Management V2 exclusively and prohibiting deprecated V1 patterns.

nodes.name: harness-optimizer.md
nodes.summary: Minimal ECC harness optimizer agent that analyzes Claude Code settings and agent configurations to recommend performance improvements, with strict scope constraints and concise output.

nodes.name: healthcare-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Healthcare code reviewer agent specializing in clinical safety, HIPAA PHI compliance, CDSS engine validation, clinical workflow integrity, and data consistency for medical software systems.

nodes.name: homelab-architect.md
nodes.summary: Homelab infrastructure architect agent guiding self-hosted service design with Docker Compose, networking, DNS, and storage, with explicit safety defaults for network isolation and backup strategies.

nodes.name: java-build-resolver.md
nodes.summary: Java build error resolver agent with framework-specific sections for plain Java, Spring Boot, and Quarkus, covering Maven/Gradle troubleshooting, common fix patterns, and structured diagnostic commands.
nodes.languageNotes: Detects framework context (Spring Boot vs Quarkus vs plain Java) before diagnosing, with separate Maven and Gradle troubleshooting tracks.

nodes.name: java-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Java code review agent covering security, error handling, architecture, JPA/relational database patterns, concurrency, state machines, and framework-specific checks for Spring Boot and Quarkus.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes Quarkus-specific Panache MongoDB patterns alongside JPA/relational checks, and workflow/state-machine review for payment and event-driven code.

nodes.name: kotlin-build-resolver.md
nodes.summary: Kotlin build error resolver agent providing Gradle troubleshooting, Kotlin compiler flag guidance, common fix patterns, and structured diagnostic commands with explicit stop conditions.

nodes.name: kotlin-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Kotlin/Android code review agent with a comprehensive checklist covering architecture, coroutines, Jetpack Compose, Kotlin idioms, Android-specific patterns, security, and Gradle build configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers Kotlin Coroutines, Flow, and Jetpack Compose patterns alongside standard Android architecture components.

nodes.name: loop-operator.md
nodes.summary: Minimal loop operator agent that manages continuous execution loops with required checks for resource consumption, progress tracking, and escalation criteria when iteration limits are reached.

nodes.name: marketing-agent.md
nodes.summary: End-to-end marketing campaign agent orchestrating audience research, positioning, landing page copy, email sequences, social posts, video scripts, ad copy variants, and content calendar creation with anti-slop quality standards.

nodes.name: mle-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Machine learning engineer reviewer agent covering problem framing, metrics design, data contracts, training reproducibility, model evaluation, serving deployment, and monitoring for ML systems.
